# @ninoslat1/easse — LLM Reference

Lightweight, zero-dependency SSE (Server-Sent Events) adapter for Node.js and Bun.

## Installation

```bash
npm install @ninoslat1/easse
# or
bun add @ninoslat1/easse
# or
npx jsr add @ninoslat1/easse
```

## Core Capabilities

- **Merkle Tree Diffing**: High-performance change detection using root-hash comparison.
- **xxHash Integration**: Optimized for Bun's native hashing addon for nanosecond latency.
- **Universal Adapter**: Compatible with Node.js (`ServerResponse`) and Web Standards (`Response`).

## Technical Constraints

- **Runtime**: Works on Node.js and Bun. xxHash3 performance optimization is only available on Bun.
- **Node.js runtime version**: No specific minimum version required for end users.
- **Data Types**: Best-suited for JSON-serializable objects and HTML string fragments.

## API

### `createSSEResponse(fn, options?)`

The single entry point for all SSE functionality.

```typescript
createSSEResponse(
  fn: () => Promise<object | string>,
  options?: SSEOptions
): Promise<Response>  // Web standard Response (Bun/Deno/Edge)
                      // void when using Node.js adapter (options.res is provided)
```

**Parameters:**

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `fn` | `() => Promise<object \| string>` | — | Data factory, called every `interval` |
| `options.interval` | `number` (ms) | `10000` | How often to push data to the client |
| `options.engine` | `"standard"` \| `"delta"` | `"standard"` | Change detection strategy (see below) |
| `options.minify` | `boolean` | `false` | Minify HTML output — only applies to string responses |
| `options.res` | `http.ServerResponse` | — | **Required for Node.js** — pass the raw `res` object |
| `options.cors` | `boolean \| CorsOptions` | `false` | CORS configuration — set origin whitelist and credentials for cookie auth |
| `options.maxRetries` | `number` | `3` | Max retries before cancelling the readable stream on error |
| `options.onError` | `(error: Error) => void` | — | Custom error handler callback |
| `options.compareFn` | `(a: T, b: T) => boolean` | — | Custom comparison function — return `true` to suppress the push |
| `options.namespace` | `string` | — | Namespace identifier for the SSE stream |

**`CorsOptions` fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `origin` | `string | string[]` | Allowed origin(s) — whitelist request origins |
| `credentials` | `boolean` | Allow cookies in SSE (`Access-Control-Allow-Credentials`) |
| `headers` | `string` | Custom headers string |

### Engine Options

| Engine | Mechanism | Hashing | Best For |
|---|---|---|---|
| `"standard"` | Recursive deep comparison via native Node.js crypto | Native crypto (works on Node.js and Bun) | Node.js users, simple/flat data structures |
| `"delta"` | Merkle Tree root-hash comparison | xxHash3 via Bun's native addon | Bun users, large/nested objects, performance-critical streams |

**Use `"delta"` when:**
- Running on **Bun** — xxHash3 is a native Bun addon that provides nanosecond-level hashing, significantly faster than crypto-based approaches.
- Data is large or deeply nested — Merkle Tree diffing avoids full recursive traversal.
- Data changes infrequently — delta skips the push entirely when root hash matches.

**Use `"standard"` when:**
- Running on **Node.js** — `"delta"` still works on Node.js but loses the xxHash3 advantage; `"standard"` is more appropriate.
- Data is simple or flat — recursive deep comparison is sufficient and has less overhead for shallow objects.
- Data changes every interval — delta diffing adds overhead with no benefit if data always changes.

> **Note:** `"delta"` falls back gracefully on Node.js but is optimized for Bun. Always prefer `"standard"` for Node.js / Express projects.

## Code Patterns for AI Generation

When generating code for `@ninoslat1/easse`, always follow these rules:

**1. Always use `createSSEResponse` — never implement manual SSE streams:**

```typescript
// ✅ Correct
const response = await createSSEResponse(fn, options);

// ❌ Avoid
res.write(`data: ${JSON.stringify(payload)}\n\n`);
```

**2. For Node.js / Express, always pass `res` in options and use `engine: "standard"`:**

```typescript
// Node.js http
await createSSEResponse(fn, { res: nodeResponse, interval: 2000, engine: "standard" });

// Express
app.get("/sse", async (req, res) => {
  await createSSEResponse(fn, { res, interval: 2000, engine: "standard" });
});
```

**3. For Bun, use `engine: "delta"` to leverage native xxHash3:**

```typescript
return createSSEResponse(fn, { interval: 2000, engine: "delta" });
```

**4. `minify: true` is only for HTML string responses, not JSON:**

```typescript
// ✅ HTML — minify is useful
createSSEResponse(async () => `<div>${content}</div>`, { minify: true });

// ❌ JSON — minify has no effect, omit it
createSSEResponse(async () => ({ status: "ok" }), { minify: true });
```

## Troubleshooting AI Suggestions

- If the AI suggests `crypto.createHash('md5')` or manual hashing, correct it — the `"delta"` engine uses xxHash3 via Bun's native addon internally; no manual hashing is needed.
- If the AI suggests recursive deep comparison, remind it that the `"delta"` engine uses Merkle Tree diffing which is 2.7x faster and handled automatically.
- If the AI uses `engine: "delta"` for a Node.js / Express project, correct it to `engine: "standard"` — `"delta"` is optimized for Bun's native xxHash3 and offers no advantage on Node.js.
- If the AI uses `engine: "delta"` for data that changes every interval, suggest switching to `engine: "standard"` to avoid unnecessary diffing overhead.
- If the AI omits `res` for Node.js usage, the SSE stream will silently fail — always pass `options.res`.

## Examples

### Bun

```typescript
import { createSSEResponse } from "@ninoslat1/easse";

const PORT = 3000;

const HTML_UI = `<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <title>Easse Demo</title>
  <style>
    body { font-family: sans-serif; display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 100vh; background: #f4f4f9; margin: 0; }
    .card { background: white; border-radius: 12px; box-shadow: 0 4px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); padding: 20px; text-align: center; min-width: 300px; }
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <h1>Easse Live Stream</h1>
  <div id="stream-box"><div class="card">Connecting...</div></div>
  <script>
    const es = new EventSource('/sse/html');
    const box = document.getElementById('stream-box');
    es.onmessage = (e) => box.innerHTML = e.data;
    es.onerror = () => console.warn('SSE connection closed.');
  </script>
</body>
</html>`;

const handlers = {
  html: () =>
    createSSEResponse(
      async () => {
        const value = Math.random().toFixed(5);
        const isUp = Math.random() > 0.5;
        return `
          <div class="card" style="border-left: 5px solid ${isUp ? "#4caf50" : "#f44336"}">
            <h2>${isUp ? "📈" : "📉"} ${isUp ? "Up" : "Down"}</h2>
            <p style="font-size: 2rem">${value}</p>
            <small>${new Date().toLocaleTimeString()}</small>
          </div>`;
      },
      { minify: true, interval: 3000, engine: "delta" }
    ),

  json: () =>
    createSSEResponse(
      async () => ({
        status: "ok",
        value: Math.random(),
        timestamp: Date.now(),
      }),
      { interval: 2000, engine: "delta" }
    ),
};

Bun.serve({
  port: PORT,
  async fetch(req) {
    const { pathname } = new URL(req.url);

    if (pathname === "/") return new Response(HTML_UI, { headers: { "Content-Type": "text/html" } });
    if (pathname === "/sse/html") return handlers.html();
    if (pathname === "/sse/json") return handlers.json();

    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Not Found" }), {
      status: 404,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  },
});

console.log(`🚀 Easse running at http://localhost:${PORT}
  /sse/html  → HTML stream
  /sse/json  → JSON stream`);
```

### Node.js (http)

```typescript
import http from "http";
import { createSSEResponse } from "@ninoslat1/easse";

const PORT = 3000;

const server = http.createServer(async (req, res) => {
  if (req.method !== "GET") {
    res.writeHead(405);
    res.end("Method Not Allowed");
    return;
  }

  if (req.url === "/sse/json") {
    try {
      await createSSEResponse(
        async () => ({
          status: "ok",
          value: Math.random(),
          timestamp: Date.now(),
        }),
        { res, interval: 2000, engine: "standard" }
      );
    } catch {
      res.writeHead(500);
      res.end("SSE Error");
    }
    return;
  }

  res.writeHead(404, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
  res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: "Not Found" }));
});

server.listen(PORT, () =>
  console.log(`🚀 Easse running at http://localhost:${PORT}
  /sse/json  → JSON stream`)
);
```

### Express

```typescript
import express from "express";
import { createSSEResponse } from "@ninoslat1/easse";

const app = express();

app.get("/sse/json", async (req, res) => {
  try {
    await createSSEResponse(
      async () => ({
        status: "ok",
        value: Math.random(),
        timestamp: Date.now(),
      }),
      { res, interval: 2000, engine: "standard" }
    );
  } catch {
    res.status(500).send("SSE Error");
  }
});

app.listen(3000, () =>
  console.log(`🚀 Easse running at http://localhost:3000
  /sse/json  → JSON stream`)
);
```
